title: Monitored deployment before training use
decision: Deploy exit classifier in monitored mode and collect real classification evidence before using classifier outputs as ground truth for model training
rationale: Sprint 9 validation is rule-based and unit-test validation, not real-world classification validation. Need to measure precision, recall, false sell rate, false transfer rate, and unknown exit rate on actual transactions before trusting classifications as labels.
consequences: Classifier outputs are observational only during monitoring period
No automated training pipelines until validation complete
Manual review required to build labelled dataset
Production deployment is controlled rollout not full launch

title: Alert thresholds for classifier anomalies
decision: Configure alerts for: UNKNOWN_EXIT >20%, DEX_SELL proportion shift >2σ, LP actions classified as sell, low-confidence events producing PnL
rationale: These thresholds catch the most dangerous failure modes: high unknown rate indicates incomplete taxonomy, distribution shifts indicate data drift, LP misclassification violates hard rules, low-confidence PnL violates reliability requirements.
consequences: Operations team needs to respond to alerts
Thresholds may need tuning after initial deployment
False alerts possible during low-volume periods
